# Distributed Systems with Node.js (Thomas Hunter Ii)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [Why Distributed?](.roo/vendor/node/distributed-systems-with-node-js-thomas-hunter-ii/01-why-distributed.md): Architecting, generating, refactoring, or debugging Node.js backend services, specifically focusing on asynchronous logic, event loop management, concurrency, cross-process communication, and distributed system design.
    - [Protocols](.roo/vendor/node/distributed-systems-with-node-js-thomas-hunter-ii/02-protocols.md): Designing, implementing, refactoring, or reviewing network protocols, APIs (REST, GraphQL, gRPC), inter-service communication, payload serialization, HTTP server configurations (including TLS and compression), or schema definitions in a Node.js distributed systems environment.
    - [Scaling](.roo/vendor/node/distributed-systems-with-node-js-thomas-hunter-ii/03-scaling.md): Scaling Node.js applications, configuring high availability (HA), setting up load balancers or reverse proxies (specifically HAProxy), implementing health checks, managing connection limits/back pressure, or benchmarking and load-testing Node.js services (using tools like Autocannon).
    - [Observability](.roo/vendor/node/distributed-systems-with-node-js-thomas-hunter-ii/04-observability.md): Node.js application observability, monitoring, logging, metrics collection, distributed request tracing, health checks, alerting, or diagnosing remote service behaviors.
    - [Containers](.roo/vendor/node/distributed-systems-with-node-js-thomas-hunter-ii/05-containers.md): Containerizing Node.js applications, writing or modifying Dockerfiles, configuring docker-compose.yml files, optimizing Docker image sizes, deploying applications via Docker Registries, and designing container orchestration for local development or production.
    - [Deployments](.roo/vendor/node/distributed-systems-with-node-js-thomas-hunter-ii/06-deployments.md): Configuring Continuous Integration (CI) pipelines, writing application deployment scripts, implementing automated test suites (Unit, Integration, and Code Coverage), calculating or enforcing Semantic Versioning (SemVer), resolving Node.js module loading issues, or configuring/publishing npm packages.
    - [Container Orchestration](.roo/vendor/node/distributed-systems-with-node-js-thomas-hunter-ii/07-container-orchestration.md): Container Orchestration, specifically defining, managing, scaling, and deploying stateless Node.js applications using Kubernetes (K8s), Minikube, Kubectl, and declarative YAML configuration files.
    - [Resilience](.roo/vendor/node/distributed-systems-with-node-js-thomas-hunter-ii/08-resilience.md): Node.js backend services, handling process lifecycle events, managing database connections, writing schema migrations, implementing caching mechanisms, performing network requests, or implementing error and retry logic.
    - [Distributed Primitives](.roo/vendor/node/distributed-systems-with-node-js-thomas-hunter-ii/09-distributed-primitives.md): Designing distributed system architectures, implementing caching, managing state across multiple Node.js instances, creating distributed primitives (counters, locks, queues, rate limiters, ID generators), or writing integrations with Redis (specifically using the `ioredis` package).
    - [Security](.roo/vendor/node/distributed-systems-with-node-js-thomas-hunter-ii/10-security.md): Tasks related to Node.js application security, configuration management, secrets handling, dependency auditing, npm package upgrades, Node.js version migrations, or HTTP request payload parsing.
